| | For anyone that Googled “joe biden” on Tuesday, our ActBlue link was the first result.

Sitting comfortably above his news stories and search results, our ads declared: “In 2008, Senator Mike Gravel took on ‘arrogant’ Joe Biden — he’s ready to do it again.”
[You noticed](https://mikegravel.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4840f01d672bb468321c6d3b8&id=c1ee9e0c80&e=85f7472d66). [The media noticed](https://mikegravel.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4840f01d672bb468321c6d3b8&id=f2a35864ab&e=85f7472d66). People donated SPECIFICALLY BECAUSE we have the most brazen Presidential marketing strategy out there.

And we have many more ideas on how to educate, advocate, and make potential donors double-take. [**Alas, it takes dollars to dunk. **](https://mikegravel.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4840f01d672bb468321c6d3b8&id=d1ad1793f8&e=85f7472d66) At the end of the day, we need 65,000 individual donors to make it to that debate stage. And if each person sends $1, that’s great! But the cost of promotion and generating media interest adds up to more than $65k. Consider giving another donation to Mike Gravel 2020. And encourage as many people as you can to join the cause. At least with our campaign, you’ll know exactly where your money is going. **[](https://mikegravel.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4840f01d672bb468321c6d3b8&id=ac02e8c4c2&e=85f7472d66)**

name: John Hickenlooper
subject: Drinks on me?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hickenlooper 2020
body:
Chip in now to be automatically entered for a chance to meet me on the campaign trail for a beer and an in-depth conversation at a local brewery--travel and hotel included. | | | [](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/6377165f/691c9bd/6c78fc19/11a4860e/2003601813/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-65kdonors?refcode=em_template")
---
| Friend,

There's a common question when it comes to picking a presidential candidate: _Who can you have a beer with?_

In honor of American Craft Beer Week, I'd like to up the ante. I was the first brewer elected governor of a U.S. state since Sam Adams in 1794, and now I'm running for president and am ready to actually pour you a cold one.

**Chip in now to be automatically entered for a chance to meet me on the campaign trail for a beer and an in-depth conversation at a local brewery --travel and hotel included.** **[](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/6377165f/691c9bd/6c78fc19/11a4860f/2003601813/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-grab-a-beer-with-john?refcode=em_fr_20190516_JH_20190516-fr-JH-contestbump-nop_nop_contribbutton&amount=1&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=")** Before I became Mayor of Denver and Governor of Colorado, I spent 15 years owning and operating breweries, so I've got a lot to say about beer, bringing people together, and actually getting things done. So I want to sit down with you on the campaign trail, and share a round--learning about your family, your job, your challenges, your aspirations.

I can't wait to chat in person with you about how to move America forward--and the drinks are on me. [**_You have through Monday to enter -- so chip in now for your chance to win._**](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/6377165f/691c9bd/6c78fc19/11a4860c/2003601813/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-grab-a-beer-with-john?refcode=em_fr_20190516_JH_20190516-fr-JH-contestbump-nop_nop_l1&amount=1&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=")

Can't wait to see you,
John **[](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/6377165f/691c9bd/6c78fc19/11a4860d/2003601813/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-grab-a-beer-with-john?refcode=em_fr_20190516_JH_20190516-fr-JH-contestbump-nop_nop_contribbutton2&amount=1&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=")**

No purchase, payment, or contribution necessary to enter or win. Contributing will not improve chances of winning. Void where prohibited. Entries must be received by **May 21st, 2019**. Enter by contributing [**_here_**](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/6377165f/691c9bd/6c78fc19/11a4860a/2003601813/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-grab-a-beer-with-john?source=rd_contestsignup"), or click [**_here_**](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/6377165f/691c9bd/6c78fc19/11a4860b/2003601813/VEsA/ "https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/s/meet-john-on-the-campaign-trail") to enter without contributing. Three (3) winners will receive the following prize: **Round trip travel to the event city, one (1) night hotel accommodations, one (1) brewery tour, and one (1) meal with drinks** (approximate retail value of prize: **$1,500**). Odds of winning depend on number of entries received. All prizes will be awarded. Promotion open only to U.S. citizens, or lawful permanent U.S. residents who are legal residents of 50 United States, District of Columbia and Puerto Rico and 21 or older. Promotion subject to [**_Official Rules_**](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/6377165f/691c9bd/6c78fc19/11a48608/2003601813/VEsB/ "https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/content/have-a-beer-with-hickenlooper-contest-rules/") and additional restrictions on eligibility. Sponsor: Hickenlooper 2020, PO Box 6377, Denver, CO 80206.

---

Keep us company on the campaign trail by joining us on [_**Facebook**_](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/6377165f/691c9bd/6c78fc19/11a48606/2003601813/VEsP/ "https://www.facebook.com/JohnHickenlooper/"), [_**Twitter**_](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/6377165f/691c9bd/6c78fc19/11a48607/2003601813/VEsHBQ/ "https://twitter.com/hickenlooper"), [_**Instagram**_](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/6377165f/691c9bd/6c78fc19/11a48604/2003601813/VEsHBA/ "https://www.instagram.com/johnhickenlooper/"), and [_**YouTube**_](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/6377165f/691c9bd/6c78fc19/11a48605/2003601813/VEsHBw/ "https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC82O0Byp_ny13O0LkO1UpUg/featured").
We love staying connected to you--but if at any point you'd like to opt out, you can do so right [here](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/6377165f/691c9bd/6c78fc19/11a48602/2003601813/VEsHBg/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com").
If we're not reaching you at the correct email address, please [_**update your information here**_](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/6377165f/691c9bd/6c78fc19/11a48609/2003601813/VEsHAQ/ "https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/s/join-in").

Dan

name: Adam Edelen
subject: It’s about where we finish, not where we started
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Edelen Holland 2019
body:
|
---

| | Hey,

My dad always taught me it doesn’t matter where you start in life, it’s about where you finish. We might not have started as the frontrunner in this race, but we’ve got the momentum now and we’re going to win this Tuesday.

You’ve been so supportive throughout this race, but it all comes down to this — **Primary Day is Tuesday, May 21.**

[**_It would be such an honor for me to have your vote. name: My Faith Votes
subject: SCOTUS Watch
body:
| | Critical updates from the current session of the Supreme Court
---
[ ](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66ie-EuEwprJECnklQBd9pnr0oT_KQ5AG8gbr0HbrBqP7MlwwyDYnyPCvl_5BRmfOJpg&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190517com01&n=1)
| The Supreme Court concluded hearing oral arguments for cases in April, and this month they have begun issuing their decisions. Rulings were announced in three cases on Monday, May 13.
#### Apple, Inc. v. Pepper
By a decision of 5-4, Justice Brett Kavanaugh cast the deciding vote permitting iPhone users to bring antitrust lawsuits against the tech giant Apple. The plaintiffs believed Apple was running an unlawful monopoly by programming their iPhones to only download apps inside of the Apple store and mandating the pricing scale on apps. App developers must pay Apple a 30 percent commission, and in turn, those costs are passed down to the consumer, a cost the plaintiff believes is not lawful since there is no competition in the app market controlled by Apple. In this decision, Kavanaugh voted with the liberal block on the bench, Justice Ginsburg, Justice Breyer, Justice Sotomayor, and Justice Kagan. The dissenting votes were cast by Justice Gorsuch, Chief Justice Roberts, Justice Thomas, and Justice Alito. In Kavanaugh’s opinion, he wrote, “The plaintiffs seek to hold retailers to account if the retailers engage in unlawful anticompetitive conduct that harms consumers who purchase from those retailers. That is why we have antitrust law.” On the other side of the issue, Justice Gorsuch wrote the dissenting opinion, “No antitrust reason exists to treat these contractual arrangements differently, and doing so will only induce firms to abandon their preferred — and presumably more efficient — distribution arrangements in favor of less efficient ones, all so they might avoid an arbitrary legal rule.” This Supreme Court decision in no way decides this issue, but instead now permits iPhone users to bring antitrust lawsuits against Apple.
#### Cochise Consultancy, Inc. v. United States ex rel. Hunt
In a unanimous decision, the Supreme Court ruled against defense contractor Cochise Consultancy. Claims were brought against Cochise Consultancy by relator Billy Joe Hunt for allegedly defrauding the federal government from January 2006 - January 2007 when Cochise Consultancy was contracted to clean up munitions by Iraqi forces. However, because Hunt’s lawsuit was after the False Claims Act six-year limitation period, district court decisions were inconclusive on the case. The Supreme Court determined that the second statute of limitations adopted by Congress in 1986 applies to “[a realtor-initiated suite in which the United States declines to intervene](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=dw19u388pVqd5EnvMpv9a0EojfBdN8n2n9io2InDee7qZxAqINNMNSQWwGwmdNH5swOu41oXxb85jvABBmoo5ZjszBTiQxoyfdtSs_Tj0lrcFNiFiD7k1fGOB0-U2Qu_xBNTiCpYKz9AWpBB97NHM0Dld0Bemr_ScVCufKR4E1I&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190517com01&n=2).”
#### Franchise Tax Board of California v. Hyatt
The most critical decision this month is the Supreme Court’s 5-4 decision to overturn a 40-year precedent of Nevada v. Hall which has long stipulated that states lack sovereign immunity in another state’s courts. The decision affirms the conservative constitutional perspective on the issue, holding to the 11th Amendment, and signals to the nation that the 5-4 conservative majority on the bench is not afraid to overturn historical precedents. The case involved Gilbert Hyatt, a microchip inventor, who used a Nevada court to sue the California Franchise Tax Board over the issue of whether he had failed to pay in full his California income taxes. The Nevada court ruled in Mr. Hyatt’s favor to a tune of $490 million judgment cost, but the California court refused to pay Mr. Hyatt because they stated it was unconstitutional for a Nevada court to sue another state. In 2016, the case was set to be decided by the Supreme Court, but due to Justice Scalia’s passing, the court failed to reach a majority decision. Justice Breyer wrote the dissenting opinion and stated, “Today’s decision can only cause one to wonder which cases the Court will overrule next.” Many people believe this decision proves that the conservative majority on the bench would not be afraid to overturn other Supreme Court precedents related to the sanctity of life and religious liberty.

clean_body: | | Critical updates from the current session of the Supreme Court | The Supreme Court concluded hearing oral arguments for cases in April, and this month they have begun issuing their decisions. Rulings were announced in three cases on Monday, May 13. Apple, Inc. v. Pepper By a decision of 5-4, Justice Brett Kavanaugh cast the deciding vote permitting iPhone users to bring antitrust lawsuits against the tech giant Apple. The plaintiffs believed Apple was running an unlawful monopoly by programming their iPhones to only download apps inside of the Apple store and mandating the pricing scale on apps. App developers must pay Apple a 30 percent commission, and in turn, those costs are passed down to the consumer, a cost the plaintiff believes is not lawful since there is no competition in the app market controlled by Apple. In this decision, Kavanaugh voted with the liberal block on the bench, Justice Ginsburg, Justice Breyer, Justice Sotomayor, and Justice Kagan. The dissenting votes were cast by Justice Gorsuch, Chief Justice Roberts, Justice Thomas, and Justice Alito. In Kavanaugh’s opinion, he wrote, “The plaintiffs seek to hold retailers to account if the retailers engage in unlawful anticompetitive conduct that harms consumers who purchase from those retailers. That is why we have antitrust law.” On the other side of the issue, Justice Gorsuch wrote the dissenting opinion, “No antitrust reason exists to treat these contractual arrangements differently, and doing so will only induce firms to abandon their preferred — and presumably more efficient — distribution arrangements in favor of less efficient ones, all so they might avoid an arbitrary legal rule.” This Supreme Court decision in no way decides this issue, but instead now permits iPhone users to bring antitrust lawsuits against Apple. Cochise Consultancy, Inc. v. United States ex rel. Hunt In a unanimous decision, the Supreme Court ruled against defense contractor Cochise Consultancy. Claims were brought against Cochise Consultancy by relator Billy Joe Hunt for allegedly defrauding the federal government from January 2006 - January 2007 when Cochise Consultancy was contracted to clean up munitions by Iraqi forces. However, because Hunt’s lawsuit was after the False Claims Act six-year limitation period, district court decisions were inconclusive on the case. The Supreme Court determined that the second statute of limitations adopted by Congress in 1986 applies to “.” Franchise Tax Board of California v. Hyatt The most critical decision this month is the Supreme Court’s 5-4 decision to overturn a 40-year precedent of Nevada v. Hall which has long stipulated that states lack sovereign immunity in another state’s courts. The decision affirms the conservative constitutional perspective on the issue, holding to the 11th Amendment, and signals to the nation that the 5-4 conservative majority on the bench is not afraid to overturn historical precedents. The case involved Gilbert Hyatt, a microchip inventor, who used a Nevada court to sue the California Franchise Tax Board over the issue of whether he had failed to pay in full his California income taxes. The Nevada court ruled in Mr. Hyatt’s favor to a tune of $490 million judgment cost, but the California court refused to pay Mr. Hyatt because they stated it was unconstitutional for a Nevada court to sue another state. In 2016, the case was set to be decided by the Supreme Court, but due to Justice Scalia’s passing, the court failed to reach a majority decision. Justice Breyer wrote the dissenting opinion and stated, “Today’s decision can only cause one to wonder which cases the Court will overrule next.” Many people believe this decision proves that the conservative majority on the bench would not be afraid to overturn other Supreme Court precedents related to the sanctity of life and religious liberty. * ©2019 My Faith Votes 10940 S Parker, 640, Parker, CO 80134

More than two years after the 2016 election, it is unacceptable that we don't know how badly Russia compromised our voting systems.
Our democracy only works if your vote is counted, and counted accurately.

That's why it's so disturbing that Governor DeSantis and the FBI confirmed that the elections systems in at least two Florida counties were attacked by a foreign adversary, Russia, in 2016.

Florida voters deserve to know more.

**[That's why I am demanding answers. We need transparent leadership to inform the public on the vulnerability of our systems and how they intend to fix it. Compromised systems in 2016 could mean our elections in 2020 can be subject to an even more sophisticated breach. Will you join me in demanding answers by clicking here to sign this petition?](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb35b3/9e00690/6bdacc9f/24074857/1791265611/VEsE/ "https://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/election-integrity?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=2+-+Thats+why+I+am+demanding+answers+We+need&utm_campaign=051719-es-election-integrity-pet&source=051719-es-election-integrity-pet")**

This is an attack on Florida and our democracy. And more than two years after the 2016 election, it is beyond unacceptable that the public does not know the extent to which a foreign adversary compromised our state's voting systems.

**Who benefits from hiding this vital information from the public?**

This information must be made public so that the American people understand exactly what happened in 2016, so we can restore the trust that's been lost in our election systems and ensure we do what's necessary to protect our country from any ongoing and future attacks by foreign powers.

**[Sign this petition right now to demand answers about this attack on our democracy, so we can protect ourselves, our state, and our nation.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb35b3/9e00690/6bdacc9f/24074856/1791265611/VEsF/ "https://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/election-integrity?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=3+-+Sign+this+petition+right+now+to+demand+a&utm_campaign=051719-es-election-integrity-pet&source=051719-es-election-integrity-pet")**

The integrity of our democracy is at stake.

Forward,

Andrew Gillum

subject: Stories from Forest Pines
body:
Last weekend between all the storms...

** FOREST PINES

------------------------------------------------------------
Last weekend between all the storms, we had another block walk with several volunteers in the Forest Pines neighborhood in northwest Houston and I wanted to share a couple of quick stories from our walks. First of all, we continue to be humbled by some of the great conversations and support we are getting from people in their neighborhood as we knock on their doors.

I met Maria who as we talked about the importance of providing a strong education and quality jobs for our youth, she began to tear up. What we do today affects the community we provide for our kids tomorrow.

I met Mary who makes and sells pupusas to support her son’s band program at Scarborough High School. She’s proud that her kids are doing well in school and in the workforce and wants us to provide more funding to school arts programs.

I met Manny who told me he would vote for us, so long as we didn’t forget about him and his community after the election. With these kinds of conversations, we won’t forget.

People ask me, “What will you do different?” This is how we can impact our district. Embedding ourselves in the neighborhoods that we represent and doing less talking, more listening and acting with the same level of responsibility that we expect from those in public office.

TOGETHER WE CAN DO BETTER

Join us tomorrow at 12:00 pm at the Raven Tower for some BBQ and help us kick off our campaign.

Marc

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: Why President Trump’s Immigration Speech is a Game-Changer
body:
"Even before the president did a microphone check for his immigration and border security speech in the White House Rose Garden Thursday afternoon..." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=bf6f0a4703&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **Why President Trump’s Immigration Speech is a Game-Changer**

“Even before the president did a microphone check for his immigration and border security speech in the White House Rose Garden Thursday afternoon, his critics were saying they hated his plan,” James Carafano writes in Fox News.

“Good. That means he must be more right than wrong.”

President Trump outlined his proposal for reforming America’s broken immigration system yesterday, transforming “the legal system first to one bringing in workers based on merit,” Carafano writes. “Today almost 90 percent of legal immigrants are extended family members, visa lottery winners and status adjustments. About 10 percent is based on merit. Let’s change that.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=441f8ae5dd&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “Attorney General William Barr has tasked U.S. Attorney John Durham to investigate the origins of the Trump-Russia investigation. If the Justice Department had done this two years ago, the country would be in a much better place,” Kimberly Strassel writes in _[The Wall Street Journal](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d835b2ea47&e=3ee4985eea)_. “The esteemed duo can give the public confidence that Justice and FBI actions are getting a fair and thorough look.”
---
|
---
| “Democrats like House Speaker Nancy Pelosi, D-Calif., and Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer, D-N.Y., are about to sell American workers, and the country, down the drain. Now that the trade talks with China have turned testy, they are running away from their earlier support . . . If we are to win this all-important trade dispute with our Asian rival and level the playing field, the entire country – including Democrats – need to stand together,” Liz Peek writes in [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a61525ee6a&e=3ee4985eea). “As we confront Beijing, nothing could be more important than a unified front.”
---
|
---
| “Consumer sentiment rocketed to its highest level in 15 years in early May as Americans grew more upbeat on the health of the economy and its path in 2019, according to data released Friday,” Thomas Franck reports for [CNBC](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=30188527ef&e=3ee4985eea). “Consumers viewed prospects for the overall economy much more favorably, with the economic outlook for the near and longer term reaching their highest levels since 2004,” economist Richard Curtin says.
---
|
---
| “Ivanka Trump is on a roll in her campaign to win pledges from American companies to train workers for well-paying jobs,” Paul Bedard reports for the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b64e1b45c2&e=3ee4985eea)_. “In her latest stop this week...she won a promise to help train 1 million new salesmen and women. It came during a visit to Salesforce, the cloud-based customer management system, during which CEO Marc Benioff doubled his plan to provide training for 500,000.”
---
|

subject: The election forecasters are making their assessments
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger For Congress
body:

| | Peter,

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: Even if the Supreme Court overturns or chips away at Roe v. Wade, Congress can still protect abortion rights and ensure reproductive justice. Here's how:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for President
body:
The overwhelming majority of Americans have no desire to return to the world before Roe v. Wade. And so the time to act is now. | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852fa0c/45c30650/981daf89/66997347/625449449/VEsH/ "https://elizabethwarren.com/")
---
In Alabama, Missouri, and in states across the country, extremist Republican lawmakers are trying to turn back the clock, outlaw abortion, and deny women access to reproductive health care.

Even though _Roe v. Wade_ established a woman's constitutional right to a safe and legal abortion -- and has been the law of the land for over 46 years -- these extremist Republican lawmakers don't care. And they are hoping the Supreme Court will back their radical play.

And it just might work. Donald Trump has packed the courts with extreme, anti-choice judges. Senate Republicans stole a Supreme Court seat and rammed through the confirmation of Brett Kavanaugh last year to cement an anti-choice majority on the Supreme Court.

**This is a dark moment. People are scared and angry. And they are right to be. But this isn 't a moment to back down -- it's time to fight back.

And I've got a plan.

Congress should pass new federal laws that protect access to reproductive care from right-wing ideologues in the states. Federal laws that ensure real access to birth control and abortion care for all women. Federal laws that will stand no matter what the Supreme Court does.

[Add your name if you agree, and let's fight to get this done.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852fa0c/45c30650/981daf89/66997349/625449449/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWNmFYQWxKUT09IjoiMTgzMjYiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6IjIwMTkwNTE3ZW0xLWZ1bGwifQ==/ "https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/protect-roe-v-wade?source=20190517em1-full&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&zip=18326")**

Here's what our plan looks like:

**1\. Create federal, statutory rights that parallel the constitutional right in _Roe v. Wade_.**

These rights would have at least two key components. First, they must prohibit states from interfering in the ability of a health care provider to provide medical care, including abortion services. Second, they must prohibit states from interfering in the ability of a patient to access medical care, including abortion services, from a provider that offers them.

Federal law preempts state law, so as soon as Congress takes this step, we'd sweep away the bans in Alabama, Georgia, Ohio, and anywhere else.

We'd end the political games being played by right-wing courts to try to narrow _Roe_ 's protections. And because these federal protections would be valid on a variety of constitutional grounds, they would ensure that choice would remain the law of the land even if the Supreme Court overturns _Roe_.

**2\. Pass federal laws to preempt state efforts that functionally limit access to reproductive health care.**

States have passed countless "TRAP" laws - laws that circumvent _Roe_ to functionally limit and eliminate women's access to abortion care - which are are medically unnecessary and exist for only one purpose: to functionally eliminate the ability of women to access abortion services.

A bill already proposed in Congress, The Women's Health Protection Act, could help block these kinds of schemes concocted to deny women access to care. Congress should pass it.

**3\. Guarantee reproductive health coverage as part of all health coverage.**

All women -- no matter where they live, where they're from, how much money they make, or the color of their skin -- are entitled to access the high-quality, evidence-based reproductive health care that is envisioned by _Roe_.

Making that a reality starts with repealing the Hyde Amendment, which blocks abortion coverage for women under federally funded health care programs like Medicaid, the VA, and the Indian Health Service.

Congress should also expand culturally and linguistically appropriate services and information and include immigrant women in conversations about coverage and access. Congress must also pass the EACH Woman Act, which would also prohibit abortion restrictions on private insurance. And we should ensure that all future health coverage -- including Medicare for All -- includes contraception and abortion coverage.

**Ensure equal access and reproductive justice.**

We must undo the current administration's efforts to undermine women's access to reproductive health care -- including ending Trump's gag rule -- and fully support Title X family planning funding. We must crack down on violence at abortion clinics and ensure that women are not discriminated against at work or anywhere else for the choices they made about their bodies.

And these issues are bigger than _Roe_. The women of color who have championed the reproductive justice movement teach us that we must go beyond choice to ensure meaningful access for every woman in America -- not just the privileged and wealthy few.

We must go beyond abortion, to ensure access to contraception, STI prevention and care, comprehensive sex education, care for pregnant moms, safe home and work environments, adequate wages, and so much more. We must build a future that protects the right of all women to have children, the right of all women to not have children, and the right to bring children up in a safe and healthy environment.

**The overwhelming majority of Americans have no desire to return to the world before _Roe v. Wade_. And so the time to act is now.**

It's inspiring to see so many women coming off the sidelines in this fight -- and women must continue to speak up to make sure this conversation stays grounded in their real experiences. Men must speak up too.

And Americans outraged by these efforts should get into the political arena, run for office, and make these right-wing Republican lawmakers face the consequences of their actions.

We're going through a tough time. A _really_ tough time. But we're not helpless. We're strong. We've got power in our democracy. So let's use it.

**Our democracy should not be held hostage by right-wing courts, and women should not have to hope that Brett Kavanaugh and Donald Trump 's Supreme Court will respect the law.

[Add your name if you agree Congress should act to ensure access to reproductive care -- including real access to birth control and abortion.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852fa0c/45c30650/981daf89/66997349/625449449/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWNmFYQWxKUT09IjoiMTgzMjYiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6IjIwMTkwNTE3ZW0xLWZ1bGwifQ==/ "https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/protect-roe-v-wade?source=20190517em1-full&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&zip=18326")**

Thanks for being a part of this,

Elizabeth
| [**ADD YOUR NAME**](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852fa0c/45c30650/981daf89/66997349/625449449/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWNmFYQWxKUT09IjoiMTgzMjYiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6IjIwMTkwNTE3ZW0xLWZ1bGwifQ==/ "https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/protect-roe-v-wade?source=20190517em1-full&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&zip=18326")

subject: On the 39th Anniversary of the Gwangju Uprising
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/mikegravel/peace-for-korea?e=85f7472d66)
---
| **On the 39th anniversary of the Gwangju Uprising**
---
| | On this day, students at Chonnam University in South Korea began a pro-democracy demonstration against martial law. The reigning authoritarian regime, which successfully pulled off a coup thanks to the timely aid of U.S. strategists, caught wind of the growing demonstration and proceeded to open fire on student protestors.

As the clashes between military forces and civilians took a bloody turn over the course of two weeks, the body count grew to an estimated 1,000 civilians—women and children among the casualties.

And we wonder; what does any of this have to do with America and our campaign?
* * *
When then sitting President Chun Doo Hwan realized the student demonstrations were growing beyond control, he needed authorization to pull military forces to the school where protestors had gathered.

The U.S. approved. Chun immediately reallocated heavy artillery to the protest site in order to rip dissenters to shreds.

We, as Koreans, have not forgotten the brothers and sisters, mothers and fathers who were mowed down by heavy artillery and machine gun fire merely for speaking out against tyranny. We, as Koreans, remember the gory broadcasts of Koreans maiming their own to sustain a violent, U.S.-led regime.

But WE, as Americans must face the truth that it was American-made, American-subsidized 500MD and UH-1H choppers that tore open the bodies of my brothers and sisters.

It was the American doctrine of crushing resistance by any means necessary that gave Chun the inspiration for massacre.

It was the manufactured rift between the Korean people that empowered the military to execute dissidents without cause.

Though I was born Korean-American, the bloodshed and trauma of my people has been passed down to me through the spectre of the American military empire.

There’s a lingering temptation to look on the capitalistic success of South Korea and believe that U.S. intervention “saved” South Korea. But we cannot continue to ignore how America’s military-industrial empire has held South Korean society hostage for over 70 years.

With war criminals like Donald Trump, Mike Pompeo, and John Bolton now leading talks between North and South Korea, the world looks on and laughs at the clear conflicts of interest highlighted by the humiliating ineptitude of the current administration. Yet, we’ve somehow forgotten that every president since the 1940’s has stymied or flat-out rejected any and all solutions for peace on the Korean Peninsula, opting instead to maintain U.S. military power. The U.S. has never cared about peace and unity for the Korean people—all they see is an opportunity for more capital.

We must end imperialism in all its forms; it begins with letting other nations govern their own societies. It’s clear from the 2017 demonstrations against then sitting military regime puppet, Park Geun-Hye, that the Korean people want peace, unity, and reform. It’s time for the U.S. to withdraw completely from the Korean Peninsula and let the Korean people decide their own future. Sincerely,

Jonathan Suhr
Director of Design
Mike Gravel 2020 [Help Mike fight for peace on the Korean peninsula with $1.00 today](https://mikegravel.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4840f01d672bb468321c6d3b8&id=64039e515b&e=85f7472d66)

body:
Hi Peter – I wanted to personally
reach out one more time this week. I’m proud to continue standing
up for affordable care; particularly for Americans with
pre-existing conditions.

My team let me know we still need 65 supporters to chip in
before midnight. If we miss this goal, we’ll fall behind on the
GOP’s attacks on Democrats that are already rolling in.

body:
Do you want a president who will actually stand up to the Republican Party and the greed of the billionaire class?
# [](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/8915?t=1&akid=1388%2E150750%2E6DOX1n)

This is what global climate change looks like:
[](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/11505?refcode=em190517-1-30&t=2&akid=1388%2E150750%2E6DOX1n)
And this is what a climate change denier looks like:
[](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/11505?refcode=em190517-1-30&t=3&akid=1388%2E150750%2E6DOX1n)
This is what a national disgrace looks like:
[](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/11505?refcode=em190517-1-30&t=4&akid=1388%2E150750%2E6DOX1n)
This is what those who profit from that shame look like:
[](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/11505?refcode=em190517-1-30&t=5&akid=1388%2E150750%2E6DOX1n)
This is what a corrupt system of campaign finance looks like:
[](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/11505?refcode=em190517-1-30&t=6&akid=1388%2E150750%2E6DOX1n)
This is the person you can trust to tear down that system and take on the obscene levels of corporate greed in this country.
[](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/11505?refcode=em190517-1-30&t=7&akid=1388%2E150750%2E6DOX1n)
And these are the people (and many many more) he’ll need with him to get the job done.
[](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/11505?refcode=em190517-1-30&t=8&akid=1388%2E150750%2E6DOX1n)
**That includes you. Because Bernie cannot do this alone.**
So if you want a president who will actually stand up to the Republican Party and the greed of the billionaire class, there’s something important we have to ask you to do about it:
[**Make a $3 contribution before our next official FEC fundraising deadline? There is only one way we win this campaign and transform our country — and that is together.**](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/11505?refcode=em190517-1-30&t=33&akid=1388%2E150750%2E6DOX1n)
Thanks for stepping up. Especially at this point in the campaign. Bernie needs you, because we only have one chance -- and that is together.
All our best,
Team Bernie

body:

---
| | | Tammy Baldwin is right. Donald Trump is wrong. Cory Gardner is silent. The U.S. House just passed the Equality Act to ban discrimination on the basis of sexual orientation or gender identity. Senator Baldwin, one of the bill's sponsors, said, "No American should be at risk of being fired, evicted from their home or denied services because of who they are or whom they love." **I couldn't agree more.** [**Will you join me in the fight for equality by contributing to our campaign today? Together we can build a strong majority for LGBTQ rights in the U.S. Senate.**](https://secure.andrewromanoff.com/page/m/7a984867/33d1dea4/3b471b97/3a3a1621/1435266445/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/romanoff2020-donate10?refcode=em20190517&utm_medium=email&utm_source=romanoff&utm_content=1+-+Will+you+join+me+in+the+fight+for+equali&utm_campaign=EM051719&source=EM051719") President Trump opposes the Equality Act. subject: If it's a fight Trump wants, he's got it

And he'll lose.
| | |

Peter,
Over the past few days, millions of Americans have spoken out in support of women's reproductive freedom to make one thing perfectly clear:
**If this is a fight that President Trump wants with the American people --with America's women--it's a fight he's got. And it is a fight he will lose.**
We've seen a surge of support for our campaign, too. It's bolstered by fed-up women and men passionate about electing a president who recognizes that abortion rights are central to a woman's basic dignity and humanity.
There's never been a better time for you to join our team, Peter. And I want to see if we can keep the momentum of this week going by adding 1,000 new donors before Saturday at midnight! **[Make your first donation to my presidential campaign right now and join the millions of Americans speaking out in support of women's rights this weekend.](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f977369/2ae112ef/68aa0585/357c47be/2368289806/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5Ijoia2ctZW1haWwiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6Imp0azMxMDUtZnItZ2ZwLW5kIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjUsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCw1MDAiLCJlM3NnWVdKeVpXTjFjbkpwYm1jZ2ZYMD0iOiIifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kg-email?refcode=jtk3105-fr-gfp-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")**

name: Dr. Laura Kaplan-Weisman
subject: We can WIN Medicare for All - here's how
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY OUR REVOLUTION
body:
# [](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a4d0/bed43eb/954eb31b/75fa8ddb/170225988/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ourrevolution?refcode=email-header-OR")
Friends,
My name is Dr. Laura Kaplan-Weisman. I'm a family physician, living in Rockville, Maryland. I am also helping Our Revolution lead the fight for Medicare for All.
[**Will you donate $5 to Our Revolution today so that we can provide essential support for local volunteers taking action to move their Representatives to co-sponsor the Medicare for All Act of 2019?**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a4d0/bed43eb/954eb31b/75fa8dda/170225988/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/or-healthcare-md?refcode=em190516&noskip=true")
As a frontline caregiver, I see sick patients who do not receive necessary care because they lack adequate insurance. Others have spent their entire savings, even to the point of losing their home, to pay for treatment. This is morally unacceptable.
We hold the power to address this issue. Right now we have a chance to pass Medicare for All because we hold a Democratic majority in the U.S. House of Representatives. **But here 's the problem - only 109 out of 235 House Democrats have signed onto Rep. Pramila Jayapal's Medicare for All Act of 2019.**
That's why Our Revolution is kicking off a national grassroots campaign in targeted Congressional districts across the country to make sure every single Democrat gets behind passing Medicare for All in the U.S. House. If we do this, we can make history.
With your support, **Our Revolution will put "Medicare for All Congressional Action Kits" in the hands of local volunteers and empower them to be effective grassroots lobbyists who can move the Members of Congress we need to move to win.**
This campaign is urgent because an army of lobbyists for big insurance and pharmaceutical corporations are actively mobilizing against us. Just last weekend, industry executives invited key House Democrats to a "retreat" at a resort outside Washington, DC to convince them to defeat Medicare for All.
**[**We can 't let the Democrats we helped elect to Congress be corrupted by lobbyists who put profits over patients. We need to hold our politicians accountable. Will you chip in $5 so that we can provide essential support for local volunteers to hold House Democrats' feet to the fire and pass Medicare for All?**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a4d0/bed43eb/954eb31b/75fa8dda/170225988/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/or-healthcare-md?refcode=em190516&noskip=true")**
I joined Our Revolution because I refuse to stand by and allow the United States to be the only major country that does not guarantee health care as a fundamental right. And I'm willing to bet that you won't either.
That's why we need to join together and make the fierce urgency of this moment clear to the people who are responsible for representing us in the halls of Congress.
Together, we will win Our Revolution!
Dr. Laura Kaplan-Weisman
Healthcare Leader
Our Revolution, Maryland

****[DONATE»](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a4d0/bed43eb/954eb31b/75fa8dda/170225988/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/or-healthcare-md?refcode=em190516&noskip=true")****

body:
Today in Washington, we have a number of Hoosiers who are working to change the world for the better. But before we sent our current batch of...

View this email in your browser (https://us11.campaign-archive.com/?e=1acd9bb3d6&u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=d0df679ee8)

Remembering a Hoosier Statesman

Today in Washington, we have a number of Hoosiers who are working to change the world for the better. But before we sent our current batch of Hoosiers to Washington, there was Senator Richard Lugar, the ultimate Hoosier and American statesman who left an indelible mark in history and in the lives of everyone he met.

Richard Lugar is the definition of that Kurt Vonnegut quote, "I don't know what it is about Hoosiers. But wherever you go there is always a Hoosier doing something very important there.”

This week, we laid Senator Lugar, who epitomizes someone “doing something very important,” to rest. He always stayed humble and true to the Hoosier principles he was raised on – humility, fairness, and service. As Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell said at his funeral, “People came first. He met everyone where they were.”

Over the last weeks, you’ve likely heard several of the many achievements he delivered to us as Hoosiers and Americans. As mayor, he ushered in a new era of growth and opportunity for Central Indiana. As a senator, he made the world a more free and secure place by reducing nuclear stockpiles in the former Soviet Union.

But arguably most relevant to us here at home, as a Hoosier he represented the goodwill of our state and our fellow Hoosiers around the world.

Vice President Mike Pence perhaps said it best this week: “In my travels around the world as congressman and governor, I was always struck that right after I told people I was from Indiana, they would smile and say, ‘Dick Lugar’. Senator Richard Lugar commanded the admiration of our allies and the respect of our enemies like very few Americans.”

Hoosiers today are following in Senator Lugar’s footsteps, keeping the torch of honorable public service alive and well. Many call Senator Lugar an American statesman, and that is definitely true. But he was also our Hoosier statesman, and as Hoosiers, his presence will be missed by us all.

-Kyle Hupfer
GOVERNOR HOLCOMB'S WEEK THAT WAS

Honoring a Statesman & More Hoosier Jobs

In addition to speaking and laying a wreath at a Statehouse memorial service for Senator Lugar, Governor Eric Holcomb also had a big week for jobs announcements!
* Tech firm MetaCX – led by former ExactTarget and Salesforce execs – is scaling up its operations in Indianapolis, creating 75 new, high-wage jobs. Read more here (http://www.insideindianabusiness.com/story/40473335/metacx-investment-part-of-growing-tech-sector?mc_cid=d0df679ee8&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) .
* Cummins celebrated 100 years of operations! As part of the celebration, the major engine manufacturer announced that it’s expanding in Columbus, Greenwood and Indianapolis. The company is investing millions more into our state and creating more great Hoosier jobs. Read more here (https://indianapublicmedia.org/news/cummins-plans-expanded-operations-at-three-indiana-locations.php?mc_cid=d0df679ee8&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) .

With companies like MetaCX and Cummins making big investments in Indiana, it’s no wonder that Indiana’s economy is on fire! In fact, tech jobs are growing in Central Indiana at twice the national pace.
http://indiana.gop/battlegroundindiana?mc_cid=d0df679ee8&mc_eid=[UNIQID]

Battleground Indiana: Winning Indiana's Cities and Towns

Attention, 2019 municipal candidates! This June, we have a big training event planned for you (http://indiana.gop/battlegroundindiana?mc_cid=d0df679ee8&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) , and it’s designed to help you build the skills you and your campaign team will need in order to cross the finish line FIRST on Election Night!

RSVP now (http://indiana.gop/battlegroundindiana?mc_cid=d0df679ee8&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) to secure your spot so you can learn more about fundraising, recruiting volunteers, communicating to voters and more! Your registration (http://indiana.gop/battlegroundindiana?mc_cid=d0df679ee8&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) will include everything that you will need for the day – including admission to a reception with Governor Holcomb and Indiana GOP Chairman Kyle Hupfer that evening.

Together, we can ensure that Republicans continue to deliver results for Hoosiers in cities and towns across Indiana!

Welcoming Ivanka Trump to Indiana

This week, we welcomed Ivanka Trump, advisor to the President of the United States, to Indiana!

With Governor Holcomb at the helm, Indiana is a national leader in training workers to fill high-wage, in-demand jobs. We received more evidence of this yesterday when Ivanka Trump joined one of Indiana’s largest tech employers, Salesforce, as it pledged to the Trump Administration that it will train one million American workers (http://www.insideindianabusiness.com/story/40486262/salesforce-signs-white-house-training-pledge?fbclid=IwAR2y_zUjobrRm48dxXnJyDk4SNSLPdgKfM1oLczvJckE_y2uKks0QgpGsvw&mc_cid=d0df679ee8&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) over the next five years.

You may recall that Ivanka Trump also is the co-chair of the American Workforce Advisory Board (https://holcombforindiana.com/policy-advisory-board/?mc_cid=d0df679ee8&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) , which works to help all Americans participate in the booming economy. Governor Holcomb is one of only two governors who also works on this board.

Thank you, Salesforce, for your commitment to training American workers, and to President Donald Trump and to Governor Holcomb for your leadership!

Welcome to the Party, Interns!

The Indiana Republican Party is excited to welcome our summer interns, who officially started this week. They’re stationed both at our headquarters in downtown Indianapolis and across the state, working hard to make a difference in elections. If you see them out in the field, make sure to say hello!

Check out this video (https://www.facebook.com/indgop/videos/338806716827169/?__tn__=kC-R&eid=ARAo7dW1x_MKAehy3GYqvW0kDdDLjYeK1kouUaTkwImLFr-G2yoe7OevsxB1Xocgj6sqS0DiOPQKxBbv&hc_ref=ARREeZztUXy-VnRvltBJQoXMmsd8RVoIxwtMPd0t2ZPgEQm5_GCbsUKM4bBhViBJm6w&fref=nf&__xts__%5B0%5D=68.ARAIDyYS3KkhI17wSKJl4kshk9qIXMJ5VXFflsAYgk4JHFiIcQ4OP8MWznFhbN6nCpfxmIi54zCZbHzTYdCwwdQbgkQzyNHDoc-xfRFCmiARDO_iQ6Ngi2ogMewHIk7p9ltGPG17CpelV8fdS8MkOGy3cKvOHEYFvVEcMbN5mMGZdSXoOQRlzre5K8MjE6kTXc9f__9MyrhDgMRJHtHe0g47g7TckYotW1iRhEcpCScP1FIAfEBPfCRgIqLexraAA_geQ0N-KxmiaYXndoNV_vkHMld2Erpw7aoK7vH0jTVl2_AY8JtAwuRIMnK9BrY4KL-DxYwgnOznZ2ae_H4ieMe7mudmwA&mc_cid=d0df679ee8&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) to meet our interns, and keep an eye on our Facebook (https://www.facebook.com/indgop?mc_cid=d0df679ee8&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) and Twitter (https://twitter.com/indgop?mc_cid=d0df679ee8&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) accounts this summer to learn more about why these college students and recent graduates are excited about joining Indiana’s Party of Purpose!

If you or someone you know is interested in interning with the Indiana Republican Party, learn more about applying here (http://indiana.gop/internships?mc_cid=d0df679ee8&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) .

Upcoming Lincoln Day Dinners

May 17 (http://indiana.gop/events/sullivan-county-lincoln-day-dinner-3?mc_cid=d0df679ee8&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) : Sullivan County Lincoln Day Dinner with Senator Mike Braun
May 22 (http://indiana.gop/events/wayne-county-lincoln-day-dinner-1?mc_cid=d0df679ee8&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) : Wayne County Lincoln Day Dinner with Governor Eric Holcomb
June 7 (http://indiana.gop/events/franklin-township-lincoln-day-dinner?mc_cid=d0df679ee8&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) : Franklin Township Lincoln Day Dinner

**Have an upcoming Republican event not listed here? Let us know at info@indiana.go (mailto:info@indiana.go) p (mailto:info@indiana.go) ! **

Governor Eric Holcomb meets with Make-A-Wish kids and families (https://cbs4indy.com/2019/05/15/gov-holcomb-meets-with-make-a-wish-kids-to-proclaim-world-wish-day/?mc_cid=d0df679ee8&mc_eid=[UNIQID])

| | | Friend, my team has been concerned about the strength of our grassroots movement ahead of the 2020 elections. I'll be honest -- we've seen a bit of a slowdown in contributions from donors all over the country. name: Senator Jeff Merkley
subject: Equal justice under law? Not for all:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jeff Merkley for Oregon
body:
| [](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2aa42/6cd27893/4bf1616c/15803668/3593540998/VEsH/ "http://www.jeffmerkley.com")
---

| | **_Equal justice under law. _It's carved in stone above the Supreme Court, but not actually guaranteed for all of us.** Not long ago, I was with Representative John Lewis in the Capitol Building looking out at the Supreme Court. We were talking about the arc of history, and how it's our responsibility to bend it towards justice. His story is legend. Selma, Alabama. The Edmund Pettus Bridge. A lifetime working for civil rights. But we all know the job isn't done. _Far_ from it. **One example: nationally, LGBTQ+ Americans _still_ face discrimination. In the workplace, in housing, because of who they love.** That's why, today, I'm thrilled that the House has passed the **Equality Act **that I wrote with Representative David Cicilline, to finally make good on the promise of equal protections for LGBTQ+ Americans. **The next step? Get this historic legislation through the Senate. [Sign my petition: tell Mitch McConnell to take up and pass the Equality Act right away.](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2aa42/6cd27893/4bf1616c/15803669/3593540998/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0=/ "https://www.jeffmerkley.com/petition/equality-act/e/#firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&subsource=pet-2019-05-equality-act-email")** I've spent my life in public service on this fight. In Oregon, as House Speaker, I was tired of the inaction on a national level -- so we got working at home, passing the Oregon Equality Act in 2007. In 2009, in his final days, Senator Ted Kennedy asked me to pick up the charge for his Employment Non-Discrimination Act. I was able to pass it with a bipartisan majority in the Senate in 2012, only to see it blocked by the Republican House. And, of course, we all celebrated the terrific moment in 2015 when marriage equality became the law of the land. The Equality Act must be next. It's a critical piece of the puzzle, adding LGBTQ+ protections to our landmark federal civil rights laws, including the Civil Rights Act of 1964, which President Lyndon Johnson signed into law. I'm reminded of his commencement address to Howard University students in 1965. He said:
> _Freedom is the right to share, share fully and equally, in American society -- to vote, to hold a job, to enter a public place, to go to school._
He's absolutely right. And that's why the Equality Act is so important. **[Sign the petition: help us fulfill the promise of equal justice under the law. Tell Mitch McConnell and the U.S. Senate to pass the Equality Act right away.](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2aa42/6cd27893/4bf1616c/15803669/3593540998/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0=/ "https://www.jeffmerkley.com/petition/equality-act/e/#firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&subsource=pet-2019-05-equality-act-email")**
**_Jeff_** [SIGN THE PETITION](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2aa42/6cd27893/4bf1616c/15803669/3593540998/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0=/ "https://www.jeffmerkley.com/petition/equality-act/e/#firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&subsource=pet-2019-05-equality-act-email") |
